title: Procurement and Supply Chain Professionals
gist: A TechInsights persona page for semiconductor procurement teams, selling should-cost/teardown pricing data, lead-time intelligence across 20M+ part numbers, and supplier-risk benchmarking. Promotional, but it carries concrete data points: a $200M chip cost-overrun case, lead-time data on over 20 million part numbers, and a cradle-to-gate carbon analysis across ~100 ICs.

insights.claim: A global automaker facing a $200M semiconductor cost overrun used TechInsights pricing models and market data to gain negotiation leverage and save millions.
insights.speaker: TechInsights
insights.so_what: Quantifies the scale of chip cost-overrun exposure for automakers ($200M-class) and that should-cost data materially shifts supplier negotiations.
insights.quote: A global automaker used our pricing models and market data to gain negotiation leverage, saving millions after facing a $200M semiconductor cost overrun.

insights.claim: Should-cost and teardown-derived BOM analysis is backed by access to millions of real-world component price points across automotive, mobile, computing, and smart-home applications.
insights.speaker: TechInsights
insights.so_what: Independent should-cost data on actual transacted prices is a procurement-side edge over vendor list pricing.
insights.quote: access to millions of component price points
insights.locator: Understand True Cost Structures and Optimize Pricing

insights.claim: TechInsights maintains historical and forecasted lead-time and availability data for over 20 million semiconductor part numbers, including form-fit-function equivalents.
insights.speaker: TechInsights
insights.so_what: Lead-time data across 20M+ part numbers is a leading indicator of component-shortage stress; useful for anticipating supply bottlenecks before they hit production.
insights.quote: Our historical and forecasted data on over 20 million semiconductor part numbers helps you anticipate supply chain bottlenecks
insights.locator: Improve Component Sourcing with Lead Time and Risk Intelligence

insights.claim: A fabless semiconductor company used TechInsights tools to run a cradle-to-gate carbon analysis quantifying Scope 1-3 emissions across nearly 100 integrated circuits.
insights.speaker: TechInsights
insights.so_what: Chip-level Scope 1-3 carbon accounting is becoming operational; signals rising compliance/reporting demand around semiconductor supply-chain emissions.
insights.quote: conduct a cradle-to-gate carbon emissions analysis across nearly 100 ICs. Using automated modules, they rapidly quantified Scope 1–3 emissions
